trial.text: Does your child have difficulty paying attention in class or during focused activities?
Does your child get easily distracted by environmental stimuli?
Does your child struggle to follow multi-step instructions?
Does your child have difficulty managing frustration or anger?
Does your child experience frequent mood swings or emotional outbursts?
Does your child seem anxious or worried often?
Does your child have difficulty making or maintaining friendships?
Does your child struggle to understand social cues or take turns in conversations?
Does your child prefer playing alone rather than with peers?
Does your child have difficulty following rules or respecting authority?
Does your child engage in impulsive or reckless behavior?
Does your child have trouble sitting still or staying in one place?
Does your child struggle with academic skills like reading or math?
Does your child have difficulty organizing thoughts or expressing ideas?
Does your child require extra support or accommodations at school?

full.text: Difficulty sustaining attention during tasks or play
Frequently loses necessary items (toys, assignments, pencils)
Easily distracted by extraneous stimuli or noises
Has difficulty organizing tasks and activities
Avoids or dislikes tasks that require sustained mental effort
Doesn't seem to listen when spoken to directly
Fails to give close attention to details or makes careless mistakes
Forgetful in daily activities
Difficulty following instructions or completing tasks
Mind seems to wander easily
Takes longer than peers to complete work
Rushes through work without checking answers
Has trouble remembering information just heard
Difficulty transitioning between activities
Requires frequent reminders or prompts to complete tasks
Difficulty controlling emotions or emotional reactions
Frequent mood swings or emotional volatility
Excessive worry or anxiety about future events
Appears to be in a sad or depressed mood
Loses temper easily or has angry outbursts
Difficulty recovering from emotional upsets
Shows fear or anxiety in new situations
Complains of physical symptoms when anxious (stomachaches, headaches)
Difficulty accepting mistakes or constructive criticism
Excessive emotional reactions to minor frustrations
Cries easily or becomes upset over small issues
Shows difficulty with transitions or changes in routine
Expresses negative self-talk or low self-esteem
Difficulty expressing feelings or emotions appropriately
Emotional responses seem inappropriate for the situation
Difficulty initiating or maintaining conversations
Doesn't pick up on or understand social cues
Difficulty forming appropriate relationships with peers
Doesn't seem interested in what others think or feel
Difficulty sharing or taking turns
Appears socially withdrawn or isolated
Difficulty understanding jokes, sarcasm, or figurative language
Prefers activities or interests different from age peers
Seems bothered by or avoids physical contact
Difficulty cooperating or working in groups
Often plays alone rather than with peers
Difficulty maintaining eye contact or shows unusual gaze patterns
Talks excessively about specific topics others aren't interested in
Difficulty interpreting facial expressions or body language
Seems to dominate conversations or interrupt frequently
Acts without thinking or impulsively acts out
Difficulty following rules or responding to discipline
Engages in dangerous, aggressive, or destructive behavior
Cannot sit still or is always 'on the go'
Fidgets, squirms, or has excessive restless movements
Deliberately annoys, teases, or bothers others
Difficulty accepting 'no' or authority figures
Engages in lying, stealing, or other rule-breaking behavior
Deliberately breaks things or damages property
Difficulty controlling physical aggression toward others
Excessive energy or hyperactivity levels
Frequently argues or talks back to authority figures
Engages in risky or thrill-seeking behavior
Difficulty waiting turns or standing in line
Often blurts out answers, comments, or interrupts others
Difficulty with reading comprehension or decoding
Difficulty with writing, spelling, or written expression
Difficulty with math concepts or calculation
Difficulty with memory or retention of information
Slow processing speed or takes longer to understand concepts
Difficulty organizing thoughts for communication
Difficulty understanding or following complex directions
Requires repetition or re-teaching of concepts
Struggles with vocabulary development or word retrieval
Has difficulty with phonics or letter-sound associations
Difficulty with sequencing or understanding cause and effect
Struggles with abstract concepts or problem-solving
Often asks for clarification or seems confused
Receives special education services or accommodations
Academic performance is below grade level expectations
